LINEやメールで写真を送ると画質が悪くなるのはなぜ？劣化する仕組みと、元の画質: Why does photo quality drop when you send pictures via chat or email? This guide explains how compression causes the degradation and walks through concrete ways to send photos at their original quality on both iPhone and Android.

FS!QRなら元の画質のまま受け渡せる: FS!QR hands photos over at their original quality
FS!QRは、アップロードした写真や動画をそのままのデータで共有できる無料のファ: FS!QR is a free file-sharing service that lets you share uploaded photos and videos as their original files. No app install and no account are required: open the browser, upload, and send the displayed QR code or link to the recipient. They can download the file at essentially the same quality as when it was shot. Use it when you want to send photos without quality loss across operating systems, whether to family, friends, or work contacts.

iPhoneで画質を落とさずに送る方法: Sending without quality loss on iPhone
iPhoneの写真は「HEIC」という形式で保存されていることがあり、送り先によ: iPhone photos are often stored in a format called "HEIC", which some recipients can't open. If compatibility matters more, choose "Most Compatible" (JPEG) for shooting and exporting in the settings.

なぜ写真を送ると画質が落ちるのか: Why photos lose quality when you send them
アプリによっては送信時に「標準画質／元の画質」を選べます。元の画質（オリジナル）: Some apps let you choose between "standard" and "original" quality when sending. Pick original and you avoid the degradation.
チャットアプリの「写真」「画像」として送る方法は、自動圧縮がかかりやすく劣化の代: Sending pictures as "photos" or "images" in a chat app is the classic path to automatic compression: images sent normally in LINE, posts to social feeds, attachments in some webmail services. Forward the same image a few times and the compression stacks up, making it coarser each time.

写真の画質が下がる最大の原因は、送信アプリやサービスが裏側で行っている「自動圧縮: The main reason photo quality drops is the automatic compression that sending apps and services perform behind the scenes. A single smartphone photo can easily run to several MB or more than ten. Moving that around in bulk strains networks and servers, so most services automatically shrink files down.
写真を「ファイル」や「ドキュメント」として送る方法、あるいはファイル転送サービス: Sending photos as "files" or "documents", or sharing them via a file-transfer service or a cloud-storage link, handles the original data as-is, so the quality survives. The trick is to send them as files, not as images.
写真アプリで対象を選び、共有メニューから保存・送信する際に、相手や手段によっては: Selecting photos in the Photos app and sending them from the share sheet may compress them depending on the destination and method. To be certain of keeping the original quality, export them to the Files app first and send from there.
友人に送ってもらった写真がなんだかぼやけている、SNSにアップした画像がアップロ: Photos a friend sent you look oddly blurry; an image you posted came out grainy after upload. Anyone who trades photos by smartphone has run into "it got worse in transit". This article explains why photos degrade and lays out concrete ways to send them at full quality — with as little jargon as possible.
同じ写真でも、どの手段で送るかによって劣化の度合いは大きく変わります。: The very same photo can degrade a lot or not at all depending on how you send it.
圧縮には、見た目をほとんど変えずにサイズだけ減らす方式もありますが、SNSやチャ: Some compression schemes shrink files with almost no visible change, but most social and chat apps are tuned to "make it light, even if it gets a bit rough". That's why fine detail disappears and colour gradients turn blotchy.
多くのチャットアプリには「画像」とは別に「ファイル」「ドキュメント」として添付す: Most chat apps have a separate menu for attaching things as a "file" or "document" rather than an "image". Choosing it is an easy way to dodge the compression.

画質が落ちやすい送り方・落ちにくい送り方: Ways of sending that lose quality — and ways that don't
端末標準のギャラリーやファイル管理アプリから共有すると、撮影時のデータがそのまま: Sharing from the device's stock gallery or file-manager app tends to pass the original data through untouched.

近くのiPhoneやMacへ送るならAirDropが手軽で、基本的に無圧縮で転送: For a nearby iPhone or Mac, AirDrop is the easy route and transfers essentially uncompressed — but it's no use when the other side is on Android or Windows.
